detail_html:
Novosibirsk State Medical University (NSMU) — Новосибирский государственный медицинский университет — a public medical university in Russia known for training physicians and healthcare professionals.

Established / Founded
Founded: 17 August 1935 (originally Novosibirsk Medical Institute; later Novosibirsk State Medical Academy; renamed to Novosibirsk State Medical University in 2005).

state: Republic of Bashkortostan, Russian Federation, Russia
detail_html:
The university was opened in 1932 and for more than 90 years has successfully trained doctors and scientists who save lives and contribute to the development of medical science.
Location & Campus

The campus is located in the heart of Ufa city, a major cultural, scientific, and administrative center in the Republic of Bashkortostan. BSMU includes several faculties and a multi-disciplinary university clinic integrated into clinical practice.

University of Tyumen (UTMN), is a major Russian university located in Tyumen. established in 1930 and is one of the oldest and largest universities in Western Siberia.

SMU’s campus is in Tyumen city, an urban center in Western Siberia. The university’s clinical training is integrated with about 24 medical institutions in Tyumen and the Tyumen region, providing practical exposure early in the MBBS program.

Ingush State University, established in 1994, is a public university located in Magas, Russia. It is a significant higher education institution in the Republic of Ingushetia, offering a wide range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs across various fields. The university is particularly known for its medical program (MBBS) and is recognized by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the National Medical Commission (NMC), among others.

Ural State Medical University (USMU) is a leading Russian medical institution established in 1930, renowned its high-quality medical education and research.Located in Yekaterinburg
The campus is in Yekaterinburg, a major cultural and economic city in the Ural region of Russia. The city is well‑connected by transport and offers students a diverse urban environment.
USMU includes facilities such as simulation labs, a central scientific research laboratory, teaching clinics, and a dental clinic, supporting a comprehensive medical education.

Chuvash State Medical University is a traditional university in Cheboksary. It was established in 1967. Indian students can simply apply to this leading.

Chuvash State Medical University (CSMU) — part of Chuvash State University — is a government medical education institution located in Cheboksary, the capital of the Chuvash Republic, Russia. It provides undergraduate and postgraduate medical programs including General Medicine (equivalent to MBBS), Dentistry, Pediatrics, Pharmacy, and Nursing through its medical faculty and departments.

The Medical Faculty has multiple departments (25+), modern laboratories, an anatomical museum, vivarium, library with extensive medical literature, and clinical study bases in affiliated hospitals with over 2,500 beds for practical training.

address: Ulyanovsk State University campus — Lev Tolstoy Street, 42
detail_html:
Ulyanovsk State University is a public, research university, located in Ulyanovsk, Russia -- the birthplace of Vladimir Lenin. The city of Ulyanovsk is situated on the Volga River, about 710 kilometers east of Moscow and has a population of 700,000.

Located in the city of Ulyanovsk — an urban center along the Volga River, known for its historical and cultural significance.

The campus integrates academic buildings, laboratories, clinical training hospitals, hostels, and student amenities.

North Ossetian State Medical Academy (NOSMA), established in 1939 in Vladikavkaz, Russia, is a prominent government institution offering 6-year MBBS programs, particularly popular among international students for its affordability (approx. $2,000–$3,500/year) and NMC approval. It features 7 faculties, 40 departments, and provides practical, patient-focused training in its own multi-specialty

Why students choose KBSU:

  • Long-established public university with a broad academic portfolio.

  • Recognized and accredited at national and international levels.

  • International student support (English medium options, exchange programs).

  • Part of global education networks and recognized in medical and other professional fields.

Chechen State Medical University, part of Chechen State University, is a well-regarded medical institution in Grozny, Chechnya, Russia. It offers MBBS programs in both English and Russian, attracting both local and international students. The university is recognized by bodies like the National Medical Commission (NMC) and the World Health Organization (WHO). 

Key Details:

  • Type: Government / Public University

  • Country: Russia

  • City: Grozny

  • MBBS Program: General Medicine

  • Course Duration: 6 Years (including internship)

  • Medium of Instruction: English & Russian

  • Total Students: 15,000+

  • International Students: Students from Asia, Africa, and other regions study here.

Kursk State Medical University (KSMU) is a higher education institution in Kursk, Russia, established in 1935 as the Kursk Medical Institute. 
Kursk State Medical University is one of Russia’s oldest and most respected medical schools, especially noted for its English-medium medical programs and international student community. It has trained tens of thousands of doctors, pharmacists, and healthcare professionals over a long history of medical education.
 Academics

  • Offers General Medicine (MBBS/MD), Dentistry (BDS), Pharmacy, Pediatrics, Nursing, and related health programs.

  • Programs are available in English as well as Russian depending on the course and intake

National Research Ogarev Mordovia State University – Medical Faculty (Medical Institute)

Established / Founded:
University founded in 1931, gained university status in 1957, and the Medical Faculty (General Medicine) was opened in 1967.

 MRSU is known for its diverse range of academic programs, research activities, and its role as a cultural center for the Republic of Mordovia.

Volgograd State Medical University (VolSMU) is a highly reputable medical institution in Russia, established in 1935 as the Stalingrad Medical Institute. It has since gained university status, becoming a preferred choice for both Russian and international students seeking MBBS education. VolSMU is known for its qualified teaching staff, advanced research facilities, and globally recognized physicians. 
University Name: Volgograd State Medical University (Волгоградский Государственный Медицинский Университет) — often abbreviated VSMU.

Established / Founded: 1935 (initially as Stalingrad Medical Institute; became an academy in 1993 and a university in 2003).

North-Western State Medical University (NWSMU), named after I.I. Mechnikov, is a prestigious public medical institution in Saint Petersburg, Russia. Established in 2011 through the merger of two prominent medical academies, it is one of the oldest and largest medical schools in Russia, with a history rooted in earlier institutions dating back to 1907

Tver State Medical University, established in 1902, is a prominent Russian medical institution known for its research, education, and clinical training. 

📍 City: Tver
📍 Region: Tver Oblast
📍 Country: Russia
Tver is located in Western Russia, along the Volga River, and is about 150–170 km northwest of Moscow, making it reasonably accessible from the capital.

Lobachevsky National Research University (often referred to for MBBS/General Medicine) in Russia. It appears the correct formal name is Lobachevsky State University of Nizhny Novgorod — this university offers a General Medicine program equivalent to an MBBS/MD, and many international students study medicine here.

Voronezh State University (VSU) is a major public university in Voronezh, Russia, founded in 1918. It is known for teaching and research and is ranked among the top Russian universities. The university has 21 faculties and enrolls over 22,000 students, including many international students, according to Talloires Network.

(SamSMU) is a modern Russian medical university with over a century of history, founded in 1919. It is considered one of the largest and most respected medical universities in Russia and is dedicated to creating the future of medicine through high-tech development, preserving traditional education, and training highly qualified specialists.

"Sevastopol State University", SevGU is the largest higher educational institution of Sevastopol and Crimea. Founded on October 8, 2014 on the basis of a decision of the Government of the Russian Federation.

Crimea Federal University (CFU) is a large, public university in Simferopol, Russia, established in 1918, and is known globally for its medical education. It was renamed the V.I. Vernadsky Crimean Federal University in 2014 after merging with several other institutions, re-establishing the original structure of Taurida University. The university is recognized by the NMC and WHO and attracts thousands of international students from over 50 countries.  

  • Academic and research excellence: 
    CFU is recognized as one of Russia's top medical universities, with internationally renowned researchers and scientists contributing to its reputation. The university offers over 250 study programs and includes numerous institutes, colleges, and research centers. 
  • International student body: 
    The university is ranked among the top universities in Russia for attracting international students, with a significant number of students from various countries studying there. 
  • Recognitions and accreditations: 
    CFU is recognized by the National Medical Commission (NMC) and the World Health Organization (WHO), ensuring the global acceptance of its medical degrees. 
  • Facilities and student life: 
    The university provides modern infrastructure, including advanced technology, and offers a vibrant campus life with various extracurricular activities, sports, and cultural events. It has numerous branches across Crimea and hostels with modern amenities and security.

National Research Nuclear University MEPhI, also known as the Moscow Engineering Physics Institute, is a public research university in Moscow, Russia, founded in 1942. It began as the Moscow Mechanical Institute of Ammunition to train personnel for the Soviet atomic project and is now one of Russia's leading institutions for nuclear science, engineering, and applied physics, also offering programs in medicine, IT, and other fields. The university has a history of scientific excellence, with Nobel laureates having worked there, and maintains strong international collaborations.

Kazan State Medical University is a prestigious public medical university and one of the oldest medical institutions in Russia, offering higher medical education and training in various clinical and biomedical fields. It is dedicated to teaching, research, and healthcare practice, preparing medical professionals in medicine, dentistry, pediatrics, pharmacy, nursing, and related specialties.

📌 Faculties & Programs
  • General Medicine (MBBS)
  • Dentistry
  • Pediatrics
  • Pharmacy
  • Nursing / Allied Health
  • Postgraduate residency and doctoral programs

Far Eastern Federal University (FEFU) is a public university located in Vladivostok, Russia, that offers programs in various fields, including medicine, and is the only Russian university in the Association of Pacific Rim Universities. It features a modern, single-campus infrastructure and has international offices in China, Japan, Vietnam, and India, with over 280 international partners.  
Location and infrastructure
  • Location: Vladivostok, Russia
  • Campus: A unique, modern, single-campus infrastructure 
International presence
  • Memberships: The only Russian university that is a member of the Association of Pacific Rim Universities
  • International offices: Has offices in China, Japan, Vietnam, and India
  • International partners: Cooperates with over 280 international partners from 43 countries

Yaroslav the Wise Novgorod State University (NovSU) is the largest university in Russia's Novgorod region, located in Veliky Novgorod. Established in 1993 through the merger of the Novgorod Pedagogical and Polytechnic Institutes, it offers a wide range of programs across several institutes and vocational colleges. The university is named after Prince Yaroslav the Wise and is dedicated to advancing education and science for the benefit of individuals, society, and the state. 
Location and facilities
  • Location: The university is based in Veliky Novgorod, Russia.
  • Campuses: It features a campus with numerous buildings, research institutes, and student housing.
  • Services: Students have access to facilities such as a hospital, sports facilities, libraries, and an international student support desk.

Northern State Medical University was established in 1932. The institution has gone through several name changes, from the Arkhangelsk Medical Institute in 1932, to the Northern State Medical Academy in 1994, and finally becoming Northern State Medical University in 2000.  
  • 1932: Founded as the Arkhangelsk Medical Institute.
  • 1994: Renamed the Northern State Medical Academy.
  • 2000: Restructured and officially became the Northern State Medical University.

Key Points :
  • Founded: 1921, originally as the Medical Faculty of the Siberian Institute of Veterinary Medicine and Zoology. 
  • Location: Omsk, Russia. 
  • Status: A prominent medical institution with a large medical, scientific, and educational center. 
  • Rankings: Consistently ranked among the top 100 universities in Russia. 
Academic programs
  • MBBS: Offers a six-year MBBS program with an included clinical internship. 
  • Language: Instruction can be in English, with a bilingual program available that also includes Russian. 
  • Curriculum: Provides theoretical and practical training. 
Key features
  • Facilities: Includes an extensive clinical and laboratory base. 
  • Staff: A large staff of experienced and academic faculty. 
  • International Recognition: An MBBS degree from the university can be used to work in various developed countries, and is valid in India.

Rostov State Medical University (RostGMU), established in 1930 in Rostov-on-Don, is a large public higher education institution and one of the largest medical training and research centers in Southern Russia.

• Name: Medical Institute of Orel State University, named after I.S. Turgenev. 
  • Location: Oryol, Russia, approximately 325 kilometers from Moscow. 
  • Establishment: The parent Orel State University was founded in 1931, with its renaming to Orel State University taking place in 1996. 
  • Programs: Offers comprehensive medical training, including an MBBS course, and is approved by the National Medical Commission (NMC). 
  • Scope: As part of a larger university complex, it is one of the largest and most advanced in the region, offering various higher education programs.

Immanuel Kant Baltic Federal University (IKBFU) is a federal university in Kaliningrad, Russia, recognized as a leading educational institution in the region with a strong global reputation. It offers over 300 programs across various fields, has a large student and teaching staff, and is a major center for science and culture in Western Russia. The university also has a medical school that provides international collaboration opportunities and hands-on training.  
Facilities and opportunities
  • Campus: 
    Planning a new 29-hectare campus with research and educational buildings, conference rooms, commercial spaces, and student housing 
  • Research: 
    Focuses on research and development in biomedical and neurotechnology to support the development of the Kaliningrad region

Kirov State Medical University (KSMU) is a public medical university under the Ministry of Health of Russia, offering modern medical education with a strong clinical focus. First opened in 1987 (originally as a branch of Perm State Medical Institute), it later became a full medical university.
Students benefit from well-equipped facilities including lecture halls, a large library (200,000+ items), simulation/skills labs, and a university clinic with modern diagnostic and therapeutic units.

Ivanovo State Medical University (ISMA) is a public medical school in Russia, established in 1930 and located in Ivanovo, near Moscow. It has trained over 25,000 doctors and currently has around 3,000 students, including a significant international student population. The university features a library, a sports center, and multiple student hostels, with an emphasis on modern equipment and clinical training.

Kemerovo State Medical University (KSMU) is a major Russian medical institution in Kemerovo, established in 1955, known for training medical personnel for the Kuzbass region and beyond. It is accredited by international bodies like the WHO and NMC, offers medical programs in English and Russian, and has a strong focus on both education and research. KSMU has a reputation for a high-quality, low-cost medical education and provides a 100% guarantee of future employment for its students.  
Key features
  • Employment guarantee: 
    Guarantees 100% future employment for its students. 
  • Research center: 
    Serves as a major scientific center with unique scientific schools and innovative technologies. 
  • Regional importance: 
    A significant source of medical professionals for the Kuzbass region, with over 95% of doctors in the region being its graduates. 
  • Student life: 
    Hosts 3,800 students, creating a stimulating environment for medical professionals.

Astrakhan State Medical University (ASMU) is a public medical university located in Astrakhan, Russia, that is popular with international students for its six-year undergraduate MBBS program. The university is situated along the Volga River and offers a peaceful environment. ASMU provides students with a medical education that includes access to well-equipped laboratories, research centers, and libraries.  
  • Location: 
    Astrakhan, Russia, a city on the Volga River. 
  • Programs: 
    Primarily known for its six-year MBBS program for undergraduate international students. 
  • Environment: 
    Located in a "peaceful atmosphere" with "rich heritage" and a "safe environment". 
  • Facilities: 
    Equipped with modern facilities, including laboratories, research centers, and libraries. 
  • International Popularity: 
    A popular choice for international students, particularly from India, seeking to pursue an MBBS degree.

address: Amur State Medical University (Academy) 95 Gorky Street
detail_html:
Amur State Medical Academy
Amur State Medical Academy (ASMA), located in Blagoveshchensk, Russia, is a 70+ year-old institution established in 1952. It offers a 6-year English-medium MBBS program, popular with international students, which is recognized by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the National Medical Commission (NMC). The tuition is approximately 2.58 Lakhs RUB per year, with total costs estimated around 17-20 Lakhs RUB, providing an affordable option for medical education in Russia.

Chita State Medical Academy (CSMA), established in 1953, is a government medical university in Chita, Russia, known for providing medical education to both domestic and international students, particularly from India. It offers a 6-year MBBS program taught in English and is recognized by international bodies like the WHO and the National Medical Commission (NMC) of India. The academy emphasizes a combination of rigorous academics, hands-on clinical training, and research opportunities, and is considered a leading educational and diagnostic center in Eastern Siberia.

Siberian State Medical University (SibMed) is one of Russia’s oldest and most respected medical schools, training doctors and healthcare professionals for over a century. It offers undergraduate, graduate, and postgraduate programs including General Medicine (MBBS), Biomedicine, Pediatrics, Pharmacy, Dentistry, and Nursing.
The university boasts its own multidisciplinary hospital and simulation training centers, providing hands-on clinical experience to students.

Northern State Medical University was established in 1932. The institution has gone through several name changes, from the Arkhangelsk Medical Institute in 1932, to the Northern State Medical Academy in 1994, and finally becoming Northern State Medical University in 2000.  
  • 1932: Founded as the Arkhangelsk Medical Institute.
  • 1994: Renamed the Northern State Medical Academy.
  • 2000: Restructured and officially became the Northern State Medical University.

Pskov State University is a public university in the Pskov Region of Russia, founded in 1936, that offers a variety of educational programs, including an MBBS program for international students. The university has a diverse student body of about 10,000 students from over 30 countries, with around 197 Ph.D. holders and 34 Doctors of Sciences on its faculty. Key facilities include 22 training and laboratory buildings, 11 student dormitories, and 8 research laboratories.

Perm State Medical University (PSMU) is a Russian medical university that offers a 6-year MBBS program for international students, with programs available in both English and Russian. It has a history of over a century and provides a comprehensive medical education with research opportunities, a benchmark curriculum, and various recreational facilities. Key details include a ranking of 39 in Russia, a global ranking of 1792, and a QS World University ranking of 801.  
Academic programs
  • Program: 6-year MBBS program for undergraduate students.
  • Language: The program is offered in both English and Russian.
  • Curriculum: Features a benchmark curriculum and opportunities for research. 
Admission requirements
  • Age: Minimum of 17 years old.
  • Academics: Must have scored 50% in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology in Class 12.
  • Entrance exam: Must qualify for the NEET exam. 
Student life

  • Facilities: Offers sports clubs, a university gym, and recreational facilities. 
  • Events: Hosts year-round sports and cultural events. 
  • Diversity: Welcomes students from various regions, including the Near East, Africa, Asia, and the Commonwealth of Independent States.

Mari State Medical University is part of the larger Mari State University in Russia, established in 1972. It offers medical education to international students and is recognized by bodies like the National Medical Commission (NMC), WHO, and WDOMS. The university is located in the Mari El Republic and has facilities including multiple buildings, hostels, and a large library.  
General details
  • Official name: Federal State Budgetary Educational Institution of Higher Education "Mari State University"
  • Founded: 1972
  • Location: Yoshkar-Ola, Mari El Republic, Russia
  • Founder: Ministry of Science and Higher Education of the Russian Federation 
Academic details
  • Faculty of Medicine: Offers an MBBS degree recognized by organizations like the NMC, WHO, and WDOMS 
  • Recognition: Approved by medical councils in many countries, including India, Canada, and Australia 
  • Language of instruction: MBBS courses are taught in English for Indian students 
Infrastructure and facilities
  • University buildings: 7 university buildings
  • Hostels: 8 hostel facilities
  • Library: Over 1.1 million books and 7 reading rooms

Progress Medical University is a medical school located in Gyumri, Armenia, offering an English-taught MBBS program with a duration of 6 years (5 years academic + 1 year internship). It is recognized by various international bodies, including the National Medical Commission (NMC) of India, making it a popular choice for international students seeking an affordable medical education with global recognition. The university provides modern infrastructure, includes a hands-on clinical internship at its own hospital, and prepares students for licensing exams like the FMGE/NEXT.  
Key features
  • Language of instruction: 
    English, which is beneficial for international students, particularly from India. 
  • Affordability: 
    Offers relatively low tuition fees compared to other European countries. 
  • Recognition: 
    Internationally recognized, including by the NMC of India, which allows graduates to pursue careers in India and other countries after clearing necessary exams. 
  • Curriculum: 
    A 5+1-year MBBS program that includes a year of practical clinical internship. 
  • Facilities: 
    Features modern infrastructure, well-equipped classrooms, laboratories, and its own hospital for hands-on training. 
  • Admissions: 
    Simple admission process without requiring entrance tests like IELTS or TOEFL.

Mkhitar Gosh Armenian-Russian International University is a private university in Yerevan, Armenia, founded in 1996, known for its high-quality medical programs taught in English. Key details include its state accreditation, recognition by organizations like the Medical Council of India (MCI), and facilities such as hostels, sports centers, and a large library. Indian students must meet specific eligibility criteria, including passing the NEET exam, to be admitted.  
General details
  • Founded: 1996
  • Location: Yerevan, Armenia
  • Accreditation: State-licensed and accredited by the Armenian National Center for Professional Education Quality Assurance (ANQA)
  • Recognition: Approved by the Medical Council of India (MCI) and the World Health Organization (WHO)
  • Medium of instruction: English

Faculties and programs
  • Medical Faculty: Provides general medical education.
  • Dentistry Faculty: Offers training in dentistry.
  • Medical Education and Higher Nursing Faculty: Focuses on medical education and nursing.
    • Pediatrics Faculty: Specializes in the medical care of children.Other faculties: Also includes faculties for medical biology and medical prevention, international students, and advanced training for physicians.

Samarkand State Medical University (SSMU) in Uzbekistan is a top-ranked, government medical institution founded in 1930, offering a 6-year MBBS program with instruction in English. Admission requires being at least 17 years old, having a minimum 50% in PCB subjects at the higher secondary level, and a valid NEET score. The university has an Indian mess, on-campus hostels with separate facilities for boys and girls, and an Indian mess that offers both vegetarian and non-vegetarian food. 
Academic and admission details
  • Program duration: 6-year MBBS course, including a one-year internship.
  • Medium of instruction: English.
  • Admission requirements:
    • Minimum age of 17 years.
    • Minimum 50% aggregate marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (PCB) at the higher secondary level.
      • A valid NEET score is required for admission, with specific score requirements potentially varying each year.
  • Intakes: Two intakes per year, in September and February. 
Campus and accommodation
  • Hostels:
    • Separate hostels for boys and girls on campus.
    • Rooms accommodate 4 or 2 students.
    • Common washrooms are available.
    • No kitchen facilities are provided in the hostel rooms.
  • Facilities:
    • Guards are present, and there are restrictions on student movement after certain hours.
    • Outsiders are not allowed inside the hostels.
  • Food: An Indian mess is available for students, serving both vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals. 
University standing and accreditation

  • Ranking: It is considered one of the oldest and top-ranked medical universities in Uzbekistan.
  • Affiliation: A government university functioning under the Ministry of Health and Ministry of Science & Higher Education.
  • Accreditation: The MBBS degree from Uzbekistan is recognized by the Indian National Medical Commission (NMC) and the World Health Organization (WHO), provided the university is approved by the MCI (now NMC)

university: Fergana Medical Institute of Public Health(FMIPH)
detail_html:
• Curriculum and teaching:
    • Focuses on both theoretical and practical knowledge.
    • Includes a 5-year program with internships in affiliated hospitals.
    • Offers a modern curriculum with practical exercises, communication, and ethics training.
  • Faculty and infrastructure:
    • Has approximately 147 professors and 19 departments.
    • Provides modern infrastructure and clinical training opportunities.
  • Student life:
    • Attracts a diverse international student body.
    • Offers research opportunities.
  • English-medium instruction: The institute provides English-medium instruction.
  • Ranking: Has received national recognition and is ranked among the top medical institutions in Uzbekistan.
  • Accessibility: The institute has wheelchair-accessible facilities. 
International student support 
  • Accommodation: Provides separate hostel facilities for boys and girls with amenities like high-speed internet, laundry services, and 24/7 security.
  • Food: Indian food is available in the mess.
  • Support: Offers support services such as academic counseling, language assistance, and cultural orientation. 
Admission process

  1. Fill out the application form on the official website.
  2. Submit required documents for evaluation.
  3. Receive an admission letter upon acceptance.
  4. Apply for a student visa with the admission letter.
  5. Pay the tuition fees.
  6. Plan your travel and join the university.

• Name: Tashkent Medical Academy (formerly Tashkent State Medical University)
  • Location: Tashkent, the capital of Uzbekistan
  • Program: MBBS with a duration of 6 years
  • Language: English
  • Eligibility:
    • Qualifying NEET score
      • Minimum 50% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, and BiologyMinimum age of 17 years
  • Accreditation: Recognized by the NMC and WHO
  • Structure: Includes six faculties, 52 departments, and a multidisciplinary clinic. There are also three branch campuses in other cities.
  • Fees: Total MBBS fees are approximately INR 25.07 Lacs for the 2025 session.
  • Additional information:
    • IELTS or TOEFL are not required.
    • The degree is recognized by the NMC for practice in India.
    • The university has an accessible entrance and parking lot.

address: Yu. Otabekov 1, Andijan City, Andijan Region, Uzbekistan
detail_html:
•  Student life and facilities
    • Infrastructure: Includes state-of-the-art infrastructure and an Indian mess. 
    • Accessibility: Has wheelchair-accessible entrances and parking.
    • Clinical training: The MBBS program includes a five-year course, followed by one year of clinical training and a one-year internship. 
    Admissions for Indian students
    • Eligibility: 
      Requires 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (PCB), a NEET qualification, and a minimum age of 17. 
    • Practice in India: 
      Graduates can practice in India after completing the program, provided the university is listed in WDOMS and meets the NMC FMGL Regulations 2021. 
    • Cost: 
      The fees are considered affordable for Indian students compared to private medical institutions in India.

The University of Georgia (UG) in Tbilisi offers an English-medium MBBS program that is globally recognized by organizations like the NMC (India), WHO, and ECFMG, making it a popular choice for international students. The university, established in 2004, is a large private institution that provides modern facilities, including advanced labs, a simulator hospital, and in-house clinics. The MBBS program has a duration of six years, with the first five years covering coursework and the sixth year dedicated to a practical internship. Admission is competitive, and eligibility requires a 50% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology from your 12th-grade science stream.

address: Chargali Street No. 73, Tbilisi 0141, Republic of Georgia
detail_html:
Student life and support
  • International students: International students are a key focus, with dedicated academic managers and support services.
  • Facilities: Features modern facilities like an on-campus cafeteria, a two-floor library, and a large conference hall.
  • Practical focus: Emphasizes practical knowledge with hands-on labs and a focus on small group sizes to facilitate communication.
  • Accessibility: The campus has wheelchair-accessible entrances

Academic programs and infrastructure
  • Medical program: Offers a 6-year MD (equivalent to MBBS) program taught in English, with a curriculum based on American and European models.
  • Other programs: Also offers programs in other fields like dentistry, as well as various Master's degrees.
  • Campus: Features a large 85,000-square-meter campus with a simulation clinic, clinical laboratories, and a digitized library.
  • International recognition: Degrees are recognized by the WHO, NMC, and are listed in the World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS), allowing graduates to practice in many countries, including India. 
Student life and facilities

  • Student body: Has a large international student population, with many students from India.
  • Facilities: Provides facilities like an Olympic-level swimming pool, gym, sports courts, and an on-campus theater troupe.
  • Activities: Hosts various events such as the NASA Space Apps Challenge and other academic and cultural activities.

Grigol Robakidze University (GRUNI), located in Tbilisi, Georgia, is a prominent private educational institution known for its high-quality, internationally recognized programs, particularly in Medicine and Law. 
Here are key introduction details about the university:
General Information
  • Establishment: Founded in 1992.
  • Location: Situated in Tbilisi, the capital and largest city of Georgia, with a campus in an ecologically clean area near the US Embassy.
  • Naming: Named after the acclaimed Georgian poet and thinker, Grigol Robakidze.
  • Type: A private, for-profit higher educational institution.
  • Motto: "Scientia et Veritas" (Knowledge and Truth).
  • Recognition: Recognized by the Ministry of Education and Science of Georgia, and internationally by bodies like the WHO, NMC (National Medical Commission of India), WFME, and FAIMER.

Overview of East European University
  • Establishment: Founded in 2012 in Tbilisi, Georgia.
  • Accreditation and recognition: Recognized by international bodies like the World Health Organization (WHO) and the National Medical Commission (NMC) of India, making graduates eligible to take exams like the FMGE in India.
  • English-medium instruction: The medium of instruction for its programs, including MBBS, is English, making it accessible to a global student body.
  • Modern infrastructure: The university boasts modern facilities, including advanced labs, classrooms, and a peaceful library environment.
  • Faculty: Features a blend of experienced Georgian and international professors, practitioners, and young specialists.
  • Clinical training: Provides hands-on clinical exposure starting from the second year of the MBBS program through affiliations with hospitals. The university also has its own hospital.
  • Student life: Aims to provide a student-oriented, welcoming environment.

Xi'an Jiaotong University (XJTU) is a prestigious, comprehensive, research-focused public university in Xi'an, China, known for strong programs in science, engineering, medicine, and management, offering numerous undergraduate, master's, and doctoral programs, with a history dating back to 1896, operating across multiple campuses, and recognized globally for its research and international collaborations. detail_html:
China Medical University (CMU) primarily refers to the one in Shenyang, China, a prestigious public medical school established in 1931, known for its English-taught MBBS, WHO recognition, and training over 90,000 medical professionals, though a separate one exists in Taiwan focused on traditional Chinese medicine. detail_html:
Course Duration : Five and half years

- Pre-Clinical : Two years

- Clinical : Two and half years

- Internship : One year of compulsory Rotating Residential

Eligibility Criteria

Completed 10+2 years/A-Level of education or equivalent with minimum of 50% marks in aggregate and not less than 50% of marks in each subjects of Physics, Chemistry and, Biology (PCB) or CGPA - 2.4.

Entrance Test and Selection

Entrance Test for MBBS will be conducted by the Medical Education Commission (MEC), Government of Nepal and selection will be done as per MEC guidelines by MEC.

  • A multi-bed hospital (around 500–750 beds) that serves as the primary clinical training ground for students.

Academic Facilities

  • Education: Offers MBBS and focuses on quality medical education with well-equipped labs and experienced faculty.

Nepal Medical College (NMC) is a private medical institution in Kathmandu, Nepal, established in 1997, affiliated with Kathmandu University, known for its MBBS and postgraduate programs, modern infrastructure, and its teaching hospital (NMCTH) providing extensive clinical training for students and community healthcare. NMC offers hands-on experience through its integrated curriculum, focusing on producing competent doctors with practical skills for national and international service, with facilities including labs, libraries, and hostels. 
Key Highlights
  • Affiliation: Kathmandu University.
  • Establishment: 1997.
  • Location: Jorpati, Kathmandu.
  • Teaching Hospital: Nepal Medical College Teaching Hospital (NMCTH).
  • Programs: MBBS, BDS, B.Sc. Nursing, MD, MS, and others.
  • Focus: Competency-based education, integrated learning, problem-based learning, community orientation, and practical clinical training.

Lumbini Medical College & Teaching Hospital (LMCTH) is a private medical institution in Tansen, Palpa, Nepal, affiliated with Kathmandu University, established to provide quality healthcare education, offering programs like MBBS and Nursing, known for its scenic location, modern facilities, problem-based learning, and recognition by various international medical councils, attracting students globally, especially from India, say YouTube, Shreet Career Guidance and Hamrodoctor. 

Key Aspects:
  • Affiliation & Recognition: Affiliated with Kathmandu University (KU) and recognized by the Nepal Medical Council (NMC), Medical Council of India (MCI) (MCI), and bodies in SAARC, UK, Australia, and the USA.
  • Programs Offered: Primarily known for its MBBS program (4.5 years + internship), also offers B.Sc. Nursing and postgraduate courses.
  • Location: Situated in the picturesque hilly region of Tansen, Palpa, offering a pleasant environment, notes YouTube and Hamrodoctor.

Chitwan Medical College (CMC) in Bharatpur, Nepal, is a private medical institution established in 2006, affiliated with Tribhuvan University (TU), offering UG, PG, and DM/MCh programs with a strong focus on integrated learning and community-oriented care, backed by a large teaching hospital and ISO certification, making it a significant center for healthcare education and services in Nepal. 
Key Details:
  • Affiliation: Tribhuvan University (TU).
  • Establishment: 2006.
  • Location: Bharatpur, Nepal.
  • Type: Private Medical College & Teaching Hospital (CMCTH). 
Academics & Programs:
  • Offers undergraduate, postgraduate, and super-specialty (DM/MCh) programs.
  • Focuses on a community-oriented curriculum with integrated teaching.
  • Emphasizes research and provides facilities for innovation.

Kathmandu Medical College (KMC) is a private medical institution in Nepal, established in 1997, affiliated with Kathmandu University (KU) and recognized by bodies like NMC/MCI & WHO, offering programs like MBBS with a focus on quality education, modern facilities, and practical exposure in a conducive learning environment, featuring experienced faculty and good patient flow for hands-on learning. KMC is known for its strong emphasis on integrating knowledge with technology for service to humanity, aiming for excellence in medical training. 
Key Highlights:
  • Affiliation: Kathmandu University (KU).
  • Established: 1997.
  • Location: Sinamangal, Kathmandu, Nepal.
  • Recognition: NMC (Nepal Medical Council) / MCI (Medical Council of India) & WHO.
  • Programs: MBBS (5 years + internship).
  • Environment: Conducive learning with modern infrastructure, labs, library, and hostels.
  • Focus: Quality education, research, and practical experience.

Manipal College of Medical Sciences (MCOMS) in Pokhara, Nepal, established in 1994, is a leading private medical college affiliated with Kathmandu University. Known as the first private medical college in Nepal, it offers MBBS and postgraduate programs with modern facilities, a large teaching hospital, and a global alumni network, providing high-quality, English-medium medical education recognized internationally by WHO/NMC. 
Key Highlights:
  • Establishment: 1994, as the Manipal Group's first international venture.
  • Location: Pokhara, Nepal, known for its scenic beauty.
  • Affiliation: Kathmandu University.
  • Recognition: WHO, NMC, World Directory of Medical Schools.
  • Campus: Features modern classrooms, labs, and a tranquil environment with lush landscapes.
  • Hospital: Manipal Teaching Hospital (MTH) is a 750-bed multi-specialty facility for practical training.
  • Academics: Offers MBBS (4.5 years + 1-year internship) and MD/MS programs, with English as the medium of instruction.
  • Reputation: Strong global reputation for excellent medical education and high-achieving alumni worldwide.

Nepalgunj Medical College (NGMC) is a well-established medical institution in Western Nepal, established in 1996 and affiliated with Kathmandu University, offering diverse health programs (MBBS, BDS, Nursing, etc.) with a 550-bed teaching hospital, aiming to provide quality medical education and foster professionalism, recognized globally and by Nepal's Medical Council. 

Key Highlights:
  • Affiliation & Recognition: Affiliated with Kathmandu University (KU) and recognized by the Nepal Medical Council, listed in the WHO World Directory, allowing graduates to practice globally.
  • Programs Offered: MBBS, BDS, BSc Nursing, BSc MIT, BSc MLT, and postgraduate MD/MS courses, plus CTEVT-affiliated nursing programs.

university: Kathmandu University School of Medical Sciences
detail_html:
Kathmandu University School of Medical Sciences (KUSMS) is a premier medical institute in Nepal, established through a unique collaboration between Kathmandu University and Dhulikhel Hospital, offering MBBS and various health science programs with a student-centric, practical curriculum, excellent facilities, and a strong focus on problem-based learning for holistic medical training. 
Key Aspects:
  • Collaboration: KUSMS operates in partnership with Dhulikhel Hospital, providing integrated academic and clinical training from the early stages.
  • Facilities: Features include modern laboratories, lecture halls, audiovisual equipment, a library, and advanced teaching methodologies.
  • Curriculum: Emphasizes interactive learning, problem-based sessions, early clinical exposure through hospital tours, and regular practicals.
  • Programs: Offers Bachelor's (MBBS, Nursing, Physiotherapy) and Master's level programs, training future doctors and health professionals.
  • Environment: Known for a supportive, homely atmosphere, approachable faculty, and a peaceful setting in Dhulikhel, fostering leadership and holistic development.
  • Outcomes: Graduates are successful in various healthcare roles, academic institutions, and policy-making, reflecting the quality of education.

university: Nepalese Army Institute of Health Sciences(NAIHS)
detail_html:
The Nepalese Army Institute of Health Sciences (NAIHS) is a non-profit medical education institute established by the Nepalese Army Welfare Fund to produce skilled health professionals, offering programs like MBBS, B.Sc. Nursing, and MD/MS, affiliated with bodies like Tribhuvan University (TU) and focusing on quality education, research, and holistic student development in a supportive environment, utilizing facilities like Shree Birendra Hospital for practical training. It aims to address Nepal's healthcare needs by creating competent doctors and nurses, incorporating modern techniques and fostering a unique learning atmosphere. 
 Key Aspects of NAIHS:
  • Establishment: Founded to address Nepal's shortage of medical professionals, evolving from the historic Tri Chandra Military Hospital.
  • Mission: To provide high-quality, globally-standard medical education to meet national health challenges.
  • Affiliations: Works with Tribhuvan University (TU) and CTEVT for its various programs.
  • Colleges: Includes the College of Medicine (NAIHS-COM) and the College of Nursing (NAIHS-CON).
  • Programs: Offers MBBS, MD/MS, B.Sc. Nursing, BNS (Bachelor in Nursing Science), and PCL Nursing.
  • Teaching Hospital: Functions with Shree Birendra Hospital (SBH), which has advanced diagnostic tools like MRI and CT scanners.
  • Environment: Provides a unique, supportive setting with modern infrastructure, hostels, and opportunities for practical and research exposure.

Birat Medical College (BMC), established in 2014, is a private medical institution in Biratnagar, Nepal, affiliated with Kathmandu University, offering MBBS in English with NMC/WHO recognition, known for its strong clinical exposure via its large teaching hospital and a supportive, multicultural learning environment. 
Here are the key details point-wise:
  • Established: 2014.
  • Location: Biratnagar, Eastern Region, Nepal.
  • University Affiliation: Kathmandu University (KU).
  • Recognition: Nepal Medical Council (NMC), World Health Organization (WHO), Medical Council of India (MCI).

university: B&C Medical College Teaching Hospital and Research Center
detail_html:
B&C Medical College Teaching Hospital & Research Center is a leading private medical institution in Eastern Nepal, affiliated with Kathmandu University, focused on providing international-standard MBBS education, advanced patient care, and medical research, featuring a modern teaching hospital with extensive facilities (like 750+ beds, ICU, NICU) and a holistic curriculum to train skilled doctors for Nepal and beyond. Founded by Shri Durga Prasai, it's recognized by various medical bodies, aiming to bridge gaps in healthcare quality and accessibility in the region. 
Key Highlights:
  • Affiliation: Kathmandu University (KU).
  • Mission: Deliver world-class medical education, cutting-edge research, and compassionate healthcare.
  • Infrastructure: State-of-the-art campus with a 750+ bed teaching hospital, modern labs, library, and student facilities.
  • Hospital: A tertiary, multi-specialty hospital serving as an ideal clinical training ground for students.
  • Programs: Offers MBBS with a curriculum blending basic sciences, clinical rotations, and practical training.
  • Recognition: Accredited by Nepal Medical Council (NMC) and other relevant bodies.

Pokhara Academy of Health Sciences (PoAHS) is a leading public health institution in Nepal, evolved from the Western Regional Hospital in Pokhara, offering comprehensive healthcare, medical education (MBBS, Masters, Nursing), and research, aiming to serve the nation's health needs through skilled professionals and advanced facilities like its 500-bed hospital. 
Key Aspects:
  • Origin: Grew from the Soldier Board Hospital (1957) into the Western Regional Hospital, then upgraded to PoAHS, becoming a major regional health hub.
  • Services: Provides extensive healthcare via its Western Regional Hospital and is a key center for training doctors, nurses, and public health professionals.
  • Academics: Offers Bachelor's (MBBS, B.Sc. Nursing, Public Health) and Master's level programs in various medical specialties (MD/MS), including specialized DM/MCh courses.

Rapti Academy of Health Sciences (RAHS) is a state-owned medical college and tertiary hospital in Ghorahi, Dang, Nepal, established in 2017 to provide quality healthcare education (MBBS, Nursing, Public Health), patient-centered care, and research, aiming to produce skilled health professionals for Nepal and expand specialized services. It serves as a major referral center, offering OPD, emergency, inpatient services, and training future doctors and nurses in diverse medical fields. 
Key Details:
  • Location: Ghorahi, Dang, Lumbini Province, Nepal.
  • Establishment: Upgraded from Rapti Sub-regional Hospital in 2017 (2074 BS) by an act of the Parliament of Nepal.
  • Status: Autonomous, state-owned institution.
  • Mission: Educate future health professionals, provide quality healthcare, and conduct health research. 
Services & Facilities:
  • Clinical Services: Outpatient (OPD), Inpatient, Emergency, Physiotherapy, etc..
  • Infrastructure: Hostel, Library, Labs, Cafeteria, Auditorium.
  • Tertiary Hospital: Currently a 300-bed facility.

Vo Truong Toan (VTT) University is a prominent private medical university in Vietnam's Mekong Delta, known for its English-medium MBBS program, recognized by bodies like NMC & WHO, offering practical training in its own hospital, modern facilities, and a supportive environment for international students seeking affordable medical education with strong clinical exposure. 
Key Highlights:
  • Focus: Primarily medical education, especially for tropical medicine, addressing regional needs.
  • Establishment: Founded in the early 2000s (specifically 2008).
  • Location: Hau Giang Province, Vietnam, described as a vibrant city with cultural heritage.
  • Accreditation: Approved by the Vietnamese Ministry of Education and Training, Vietnam National University - Ho Chi Minh City System, National Accreditation, NMC, WHO, and WDOMS.
  • MBBS Program: Offers a 5-year study + 1-year internship, taught in English, following the C.D.I.O framework for practical learning.
  • Facilities: Features a 350-bed teaching hospital, modern labs, e-libraries, Wi-Fi, and separate hostels with international food options.
  • Environment: Aims to provide a holistic, supportive, and affordable learning atmosphere, tackling initial societal biases against private institutions. 
In essence, VTT University aims to be a comprehensive medical training center, providing hands-on experience and meeting international standards for aspiring doctors, say VTT University - Bright Path, Ria Overseas, and Vo Truong Toan (VTT) University - JK Consultancy.

address: Anna Medical College, Sans Souci Road
detail_html:
Anna Medical College is a private medical college in Mauritius offering a 5-year MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery) program designed especially for both local and international students. It focuses on delivering quality medical education, strong clinical exposure, and a multicultural learning environment. 
    • Offers a structured 5-year MBBS program with clinical training.
    • Emphasis on modern learning infrastructure: multimedia classrooms, labs, clinical skills training, library with thousands of books and e-resources.
    • Multicultural stude
    • nt community with peers from Asia, Africa, Europe, and other regions.
    • Location & Facilities: Situated in Montagne Blanche, with preclinical/paraclinical labs and a teaching hospital in Flacq, boasting modern classrooms, libraries, and labs with ample resources.
    • Academics: Offers a 5-year MBBS course structured in 10 semesters, emphasizing critical thinking and modern medical sciences.
    • Faculty & Culture: Features a mix of Indian and Mauritian staff, creating familiarity for Indian students, and fosters a multicultural environment with students from diverse countries.
    • Affiliation & Recognition: Affiliated with UTM and recognized by the Medical Council of Mauritius and TEC, listed in WDOMS

address: 33 Xô Viết Nghệ Tĩnh Street, Hòa Cường Ward
detail_html:
Đại học Đông Á (University of East Asia) is a private, multidisciplinary university in Da Nang, Vietnam, founded in 2009, known for its IT, engineering, health, and business programs, striving for international standards with modern facilities and a vision for regional development, popular for affordable MBBS programs for Indian students. It aims to be a leading institution in Asia, focusing on high-quality, practical education with global career opportunities. 
Key Aspects
  • Type & Location: Private, multidisciplinary university in Da Nang, Vietnam, with expansions to other cities like Hue and Gia Lai.
  • History: Evolved from a private high school, officially becoming Dong A University in 2009; its name means "East Asia".
  • Academics: Offers diverse programs in IT, Engineering, Business, Health Sciences (Pharmacy, Nursing), Tourism, and Languages.
  • Facilities: Features modern labs (IT, Languages, Skills), electronic libraries, lecture halls, and a campus with a good landscape.
  • International Focus: Aims for international quality standards, attracting international students, including for its recognized MBBS program.
  • Vision: To be a prestigious university in Vietnam and Asia by 2030/2050, contributing to research and community development.

Pitirim Sorokin Syktyvkar State University (SyktSU) is the largest and principal university in Russia's Komi Republic, established in 1972 and named after sociologist Pitirim Sorokin, serving as a major hub for education, science, and medicine in North-West Russia with a focus on innovation, research, and offering programs across various fields, including medicine. 
Key Highlights:
  • Name: Officially Pitirim Soririm Sorokin Syktyvkar State University (SyktSU) en.syktsu.ru/about-the-university/.
  • Location: Syktyvkar State University, Syktyvkar, Russia.
  • Status: A Russian flagship university recognized for advanced infrastructure and scientific development.
  • Academics: Offers all levels of higher education, known for quality education and research, particularly in medicine (MBBS), biology, ecology, and more.
  • Environment: Provides a cross-cultural setting with international students, supported by facilities like hostels and a focus on creating a friendly atmosphere.
  • Research: Home to the Sorokin Research Center and involved in international scientific cooperation. 


Key Recognition Points:
  • Government Recognition: Recognized by the Ministry of Science and Higher Education of the Russian Federation.
  • Medical Recognition: Approved by the WHO and NMC, ensuring validity for Indian students.
  • Flagship Status: Designated as a Russian flagship university in 2017. 


University Strengths:

  • Comprehensive Programs: Offers over 100 Bachelor's and Master's programs.
  • Research Focus: Strong in ecology, biology, medicine, economics, and humanities.
  • Modern Infrastructure: Features advanced science and innovation facilities.
  • International Cooperation: Active in global scientific collaboration. 


Location & Environment:


  • Located in Syktyvkar, the capital of the Komi Republic, a safe and culturally rich city.
  • Offers a multicultural environment with students from around the world.

Tbilisi State Medical University (TSMU) is a leading government-run medical institution in Tbilisi, Georgia, and the largest medical university in Georgia and Eastern Europe. It is a highly regarded university with a long history, offering various medical programs that are recognized by international bodies like the World Health Organization (WHO) and the National Medical Commission (NMC). 

  • Reputation: TSMU is a prestigious government institution known for its quality medical programs and is a cornerstone of higher medical education in the country.
  • Accreditation: The university holds international accreditations from organizations including the World Health Organization (WHO), the National Medical Commission (NMC), and UNESCO.
  • Facilities: It features modern, well-equipped facilities to support learning and research.
  • Programs: TSMU offers a wide range of medical programs.
  • Global recognition: Its programs are globally recognized, making it an attractive option for both Georgian and international students.

Yaroslavl State Medical University (YSMU) is a large, government-run medical institution in Yaroslavl, Russia, offering programs in Medicine, Pharmacy, Pediatrics, Dentistry, Psychology, and advanced training, known for its practical experience for students, including international ones, with reasonable fees and good FMGE (Foreign Medical Graduate Exam) success for Indian students, making it a notable choice for medical education in Russia. 
Key Details:
  • Type: Government Medical University.
  • Location: Yaroslavl, Russia.
  • Established: 1944.
  • Students: Hosts over 3,800 students, including international students.
  • Teaching Staff: Over 1,200 faculty, including many Doctors of Sciences. 
Faculties & Programs:
  • General Medicine (MBBS/MD).
  • Pediatrics.
  • Pharmacy.
  • Stomatology (Dentistry).
  • Clinical Psychology & Social Work.
  • Postgraduate Training. 
For Indian Students:
  • MBBS Duration: 6 years (includes internship).
  • Fees: Affordable 
  • FMGE: Consistent high performance with over 60% pass rates in recent years, thanks to practical training opportunities. 
Facilities:
  • Modern classrooms, hostels, libraries, gym, and sports complex. 
Why Choose YSMU?
  • Leader in medical education in Russia.
  • Strong focus on practical experience.
  • Good value for money (low-cost medicine program).
  • Valid and recognized degree (after clearing FMGE in India).

Kemerovo State University (KemSU) is a major Siberian federal university in Kemerovo, Russia, founded in 1974, known as a leading educational and scientific center with strong programs in medicine, technology, and humanities, attracting international students with its comprehensive facilities, research focus, and supportive environment for global learners. It offers a wide range of degrees, operates through various institutes, and emphasizes practical training and innovation, especially within its Medical Institute, which is well-regarded for its medical education and research. 
Key Aspects:
  • History & Status: Evolved from a pedagogical institute, becoming a full university in 1974, and is a key federal institution in the Kuzbass region.
  • Academics: Hosts numerous institutes (like the prominent Medical Institute), departments, and chairs, offering extensive bachelor's, specialist, and postgraduate programs.
  • International Focus: Actively recruits international students, providing support like Russian language courses and help with adaptation, with English as a medium of instruction in some programs like MBBS.
  • Research & Innovation: A significant scientific hub, particularly in medicine, with collaborations with major regional hospitals for advanced medical training and technology development.
  • Campus Life: Features modern facilities, dormitories, and numerous student organizations, promoting a safe and engaging environment for its 20,000+ student body.

Kazan Federal University (KFU) is a historic Russian public research university (est. 1804) in Kazan,offering numerous programs (Bachelors, Masters, PhD) in Russian, English, and Tatar, attracting over 11,500 international students with strong recognition, especially for its medical (MBBS) programs. Known for organic chemistry, KFU boasts a large campus, modern facilities, over 600 programs, and holds significant world rankings (e.g., QS #370, ~#450), making it a top choice for international education, particularly for Indian students.

Altai State Medical University (ASMU) is a leading Russian medical institution in Barnaul, known for training international students, especially from India, in affordable English-taught programs like MBBS (6 years) with internship. Recognized by NMC, WHO, and MCI, it offers practical hospital experience, a secure campus with diverse cultural life, and prepares graduates for global practice after clearing local exams like FMGE/NEXT. 
Key Aspects:
  • Location: Barnaul, Russia (capital of Altai Krai).
  • Recognition: Medical Council of India (MCI/NMC), World Health Organization (WHO).
  • Programs: General Medicine (MBBS in English, 6 years including internship), Dentistry, Pharmacy (5 years, English/Bilingual).
  • International Focus: Large foreign student body (including many Indians), popular globally.
  • Campus Life: Secure (CCTV, strict discipline), vibrant, with cultural activities.
  • Infrastructure: Modern facilities, strong network with hospitals/clinics.
  • For Indian Students: Offers affordable education, recognized for practice in India after passing required exams (FMGE/NEXT).


Key Details:
  • Location: Barnaul, Altai Krai, Russia.
  • Established: 1954.
  • Recognition: Approved by NMC (National Medical Commission) & WHO.
  • Programs: Offers MBBS (General Medicine).
  • Duration: 6 Years (including internship).
  • Eligibility (for Indian Students): 50% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, Biology; NEET qualification required.
  • Graduates: Has trained around 30,000 specialists.
  • Ranking: Around 5703rd globally (UniRank 2024). 

For Indian Students:

  • A Russian MBBS from ASMU is valid in India if recognized by NMC.
  • Graduates must pass the FMGE (Foreign Medical Graduate Examination) or NEXT (National Exit Test) to get a license to practice in India.

address: Lancaster University University House, Bailrigg, Lancashire, LA1 4YW United Kingdom 🇬🇧
detail_html:
Lancaster University is a highly-ranked UK institution known for excellent research, teaching, and strong graduate employability, established in 1964 and featuring a unique collegiate system on its parkland campus in Bailrigg, near Lancaster, England. It offers a broad range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs, emphasizes career development, and is recognized globally for its quality in teaching and research, holding a Gold rating in the Teaching Excellence Framework. 

Lancaster University is a public research university located in Bailrigg, City of Lancaster, Lancashire, England, UK. It was established by royal charter in 1964 as one of a group of new universities created in the 1960s. The campus spans a large parkland site and has a collegiate structure with multiple residential colleges.
Key Details

  • Founded / Established: 1964 by royal charter.

  • Type: Public Research University.

  • Campus: Bailrigg campus, 578 acres parkland site.

  • Motto: Truth lies open to all.

  • Chancellor: Alan Milburn.

  • Vice-Chancellor: Steve Decent.

  • Academic Affiliations: AACSB, ACU, AMBA, EUA, EQUIS, N8 Group, Universities UK.

  • Official Website: https://www.lancaster.ac.uk/

address: Ibn Sina Street 45, Navoi City, Navoi Region, Uzbekistan
detail_html: Navoi State University of Mining and Technologies (NSUMT), established in 1956 and located in Navoiy, Uzbekistan, is a premier institution specializing in mining, metallurgy, and engineering. It features four faculties, 22 undergraduate and 19 master's programs, and collaborates internationally with universities in Germany, Russia, and China.

university: Jalal-Abad International University Faculity Of Medical
detail_html:
• Curriculum & Medium: The MBBS program is taught entirely in English, focusing on both theoretical knowledge and practical clinical experience.

  • Facilities: The university offers modern labs, a library, and access to a private hospital for clinical practice.
  • Admission Requirements:

university: Bukhara Innovative Education and Medical University
detail_html:
Bukhara Innovative Education and Medicine University (BIEMU) is a premier institution in Uzbekistan focusing on advanced, modern medical education. It offers comprehensive MBBS and related medical programs, featuring practical training through specialized clinical departments, hospitals, and high-tech simulation centers, including hologram systems to enhance student learning. 
Key Aspects of BIEMU
  • Accreditation: The institution's programs are recognized by major international bodies, including the World Health Organization (WHO), and are accepted by the National Medical Commission (NMC/MCI) for Indian students.
  • Clinical Training: BIEMU emphasizes hands-on experience, with students participating in clinical rotations in departments like dermatology and endocrinology, along with access to modern, simulated environments.
  • Education Model: As an "innovative" institution, it focuses on combining theoretical knowledge with practical, technology-driven applications to train skilled medical professionals.

address: 4th Microdistrict, Gulistan City, Syrdarya Region, 120100, Uzbekistan
detail_html: Gulistan State University (GulSU), located in Uzbekistan, is a public institution established in 1965, recognized for its comprehensive education in natural sciences, pedagogy, and increasingly, medicine (MBBS) for international students. It offers a multicultural environment with over 8,800 students, ranked in the top 1100 in Asia.

Namangan State University (NamSU) is a major public higher education institution located in Namangan, Uzbekistan, established in 1941 and gaining university status in 1992. It hosts over 25,000–28,000 students and 700+ academic staff across 16 faculties. The institution offers diverse programs in pedagogy, medicine, economics, and biotechnology, with growing international partnerships.

Alte University, located in Tbilisi, Georgia, is a leading, modern, and internationally recognized higher education institution established in 2002. Formerly known as Tbilisi Open University, it offers English-taught programs in Medicine, Business, Law, and Technology for over 2,500 students from 45+ countries. The university is known for its practical, student-centric approach and is a popular choice for international students studying MBBS. 
Key Aspects of Alte University
  • Schools & Programs: The university comprises four main schools: The International School of Medicine, the School of Business, the School of Law and Social Sciences, and the School of Information Technology.
  • Medical Education (MBBS): A major focus is its 6-year English-medium MBBS program, which is approved by the National Medical Commission (NMC) of India and recognized by the World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS).
  • International Recognition & Partnerships: The institution holds partnerships with over 50 higher education institutions worldwide.
  • Campus & Facilities: Situated in Tbilisi, the university provides modern infrastructure, including advanced laboratories, high-tech learning spaces, and a digital library.
  • Focus on Innovation: Alte University promotes a "zenith" of education approach, incorporating interactive, simulation-based, and practical teaching methods to prepare students for global careers.

city: Plantation Providence East Bank Demerara Georgetown
detail_html:
Texila American University (TAU) is a globally recognized private medical university established in 2010, with main campuses in Guyana and Zambia, offering affordable, US-curriculum-based Doctor of Medicine (MD) and other healthcare/management programs for aspiring doctors and professionals worldwide, known for its international accreditations (like CAAM-HP, ACCM), focus on practical skills, clinical rotations (including in the USA), and integration of global licensing exam prep (USMLE). 
Key Aspects of TAU:
  • Global Presence & Recognition: Campuses in Guyana & Zambia, with worldwide offices, delivering education to many countries, with courses recognized internationally.
  • Curriculum & Approach: Offers a US-based curriculum for its MD program, emphasizing innovative teaching, practical training in state-of-the-art labs, and research opportunities.
  • Accreditation & Affiliations: Holds accreditations like CAAM-HP, ACCM, and BAC, listed in IMED/WDMS, and is a participant in the UN Global Compact.

address: 215 Điện Biên Phủ Street, Ward 15, Bình Thạnh District
detail_html:
Hong Bang International University (HIU), founded in 1997 in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, is a premier private institution renowned for its health sciences, including a 6-year English-medium Medical Doctor (MD) program. It is a member of the Nguyen Hoang Group, focusing on international standards with WHO and NMC recognition. 
Key Details About HIU:
  • Location: Main Campus at 215 Dien Bien Phu, Binh Thanh District; Health Science Campus at 120 Hoa Binh, Tan Phu District, Ho Chi Minh City.
  • Flagship Programs: Known for the School of Health Sciences (Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Nursing, Physiotherapy).

university: Dunărea de Jos University of Galați
detail_html:
The University of Galați, initially known as the Polytechnic Institute, was established in July 1974 through the merger of the Polytechnic Institute and the Pedagogical Institute. The Polytechnic Institute of Galați had its roots in the Land Improvement Institute, founded in 1948, and the Naval and Mechanical Engineering Institute, established in 1951. In 1953, the Institute of Fish Breeding and Fishing Technology, located in Constanța, was transferred to Galați and merged with the Naval and Agronomic Institutes, forming the Technical Institute of Galați. In 1955, the Institute for Food Industry of Bucharest was also relocated to Galați. Two years later, these various higher education institutions were consolidated into the Polytechnic Institute of Galați..

The Pedagogical Institute of Galați was founded in 1959 and consisted of five faculties: Languages, Mathematics, Physics-Chemistry, Natural Sciences, and Sports. The two higher education institutions, the Polytechnic Institute and the Pedagogical Institute, merged in 1974 and formed the University of Galați. In 1991, the university was renamed "Dunărea de Jos" ("Lower Danube") University, after the historical name of the area around the city of Galați.
